revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近津鹿島神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chikōzu Kashima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Chikozu-kashima.jpg |location = Matsuo Town, Chikōzu 953, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Kumamoto|ja|熊本市西区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Kumamoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kumamoto City, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude Degrees = 32 |Latitude Minutes = 48 |Latitude Seconds = 21.9 |N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 130 |Longitude Minutes = 36 |Longitude Seconds = 40.7 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP-43 |Map = Japan Kumamoto city |deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} |type = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 893 |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Kashima-no-miya |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = October 14 |Rituals = Fire God Festival |location = {{ill|Kumamoto|qid=Q199889|1=Kumamoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.806105|N|130.611316|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Chikōzu Kashima Shrine''' (近津 鹿島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Matsuo Town, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Kumamoto|ja|熊本市西区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Kumamoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kumamoto City, Kumamoto Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} and is renowned for its "'''Fire God Festival'''." == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it originally enshrined the Utomiya Shrine. In the year 893 (during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|qid=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}), Fujiwara Yasunori, a fourth-generation {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|民部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan|qq=}} official of the {{ill|Fujiwara Nan-ke|en|Fujiwara Nan-ke|qid=Q2975332|lt=Southern House of the Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara Nan-ke}}, invited a spirit from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|qid=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}} (present-day Ibaraki Prefecture) and rebuilt Utomiya Shrine into what became known as "Kashima-no-miya." Another legend tells of a divine revelation in which a white-haired old man riding a white horse appeared from the sea, declaring, "I am the Great deity of Kashima, and I will settle here." The next morning, a large stone resembling a horse was found on the shore, with the deity's image visible upon it. This stone was enshrined as the sacred body of the Great deity of Kashima.<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典43 熊本県』[[:en:角川書店|角川書店]]、1987th year 、726頁</ref> In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. Its annual festival, held on October 14, features the popular "'''Fire God Festival'''," attracting lively crowds.<ref>[[鈴木喬 (歴史研究家)|鈴木喬]]編『熊本の神社と寺院』[[熊本日日新聞社]]、1980th year 、32頁</ref> The shrine was also highly respected by {{ill|Hayashi Ōen|en|Hayashi Ōen|qid=Q5686042|lt=Hayashi Ōen|1=Hayashi Ōen}}, a {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|qid=Q855159|lt=National Learning|1=Kokugaku}} scholar influential during the late Edo period, and his students, including members of the {{ill|Revere Heaven Party|ja|敬神党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Revere Heaven Party|1=Kēshin Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11499588}} who played key roles in the {{ill|Shinpūren rebellion|en|Shinpūren rebellion|qid=Q2975371|lt=Shinpūren Rebellion|1=Shinpūren rebellion}}.<ref>[[荒木精之]]『神風連実記』新人物往来社、1971st year </ref> == deity == * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} == festival == * October 14 – Fire God Festival == References == <references /> == Bibliography == * 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典43 熊本県』角川書店、1987th year 、1189頁 * 日本歴史地名大系44『熊本県の地名』[[:en:平凡社|平凡社]]、1985th year 、522-523頁 * 新熊本市史編纂委員会『新熊本市史、別編第2巻』[[:en:熊本市|熊本市]]、1996th year 、375-376頁
== See Also == * {{ill|Shinpūren rebellion|en|Shinpūren rebellion|qid=Q2975371|lt=Shinpūren Rebellion|1=Shinpūren rebellion}} * {{ill|Shinkaidaijingū|ja|新開大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkaidaijingū|1=Shinkai-daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11504266}} * {{ill|Abumita Kizuki Shrine|ja|鐙田杵築神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abumita Kizuki Shrine|1=Abumita Kitsuki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11651340}} * {{ill|Bessho Kotohira Shrine|ja|別所琴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Kotohira Shrine|1=Bessho Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11396790}} {{Shinto 横|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|近津鹿島神社|version=64922534|comment=Imported full ja history}}
